practicals.title: Mathematics Laboratory Activities
practicals.details: Lab activities to be done from prescribed books (NCERT/CBSE) covering representation of rational numbers on number line and density proofs.

practicals.title: Geometric Constructions Lab
practicals.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
practicals.details: Construct geometrical shapes like bisectors of line segments, angles, triangles, and other polygons satisfying given constraints using stated axioms.

practicals.title: 3-D Shapes Surface Area and Volume Lab
practicals.details: Compute surface area/volume of cuboids, cylinders, cones, pyramids, spheres (including hemispheres) using computational thinking.

practicals.title: Prescribed Laboratory Manual Activities
practicals.details: Lab activities from 'Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools' covering empirical probability experiments and theoretical probability concepts.

activities.title: Construct the Square Root Spiral
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral to represent rational and irrational numbers through visual models.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to model patterns in sequences using computational thinking.

activities.title: Visualise Algebraic Identities with Geometric Models
activities.details: Determine factors of algebraic expressions using geometric models and visualising identities.

activities.title: Floor Plan on a Grid
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates to specify locations and positions.

activities.title: Discovering Euclid's Definitions via Sulbasutras
activities.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
activities.details: Describe contributions of India, Egypt, and Greece; explain the construction of a square as described in Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Paper Manipulation for Geometric Patterns
activities.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Involving Circles
activities.details: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, such as the Dharmachakra, Ashoka Chakra, and Sudarshan Chakra.

activities.title: Squaring a Rectangle Proof Activity
activities.chapter_name: Area of shapes, Heron's formula, Squaring a rectangle, Circle area, Brahmagupta's formula
activities.details: Explain the classical problem of 'squaring' a given shape using proof from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Real-life Data Analysis and Graphical Representation
activities.details: Collect, organise, visualise data to answer a statistical investigative question; read stacked bar graphs.

activities.title: Randomness Simulation Experiments
activities.details: Use computational thinking strategies, such as pattern recognition and simulation, to model random experiments.

projects.title: Historical Approximations of Pi
projects.chapter_name: Area of shapes, Heron's formula, Squaring a rectangle, Circle area, Brahmagupta's formula
projects.details: List historical approximations to π from Archimedes, Aryabhata, and Zu Chongzhi; explain ancient civilisations' methods for circle area.

projects.title: Applications of Circles in Transport and Technology
projects.details: Explain how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology; identify cultural motifs.

projects.title: Statistical Investigation Project
projects.details: Apply computational thinking strategies to analyse real-life data, create appropriate graphical representations (stacked bar graphs), and interpret mean, median, mode for decision-making.

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.
Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria.
Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

competency_weightage: Competencies are integrated across all units (CG-1 to CG-11) with specific focus on computational thinking, logical reasoning, and real-life application.
No explicit numerical weightage per competency is provided in the source text; assessment focuses on holistic competency development.

practicals.title: Mathematics Laboratory Activities
practicals.details: Lab activities to be done from prescribed books (NCERT/CBSE) covering representation of rational numbers on number line and density proofs.

practicals.title: Geometric Constructions Lab
practicals.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
practicals.details: Construct geometrical shapes like bisectors of line segments, angles, triangles, and other polygons satisfying given constraints using stated axioms.

practicals.title: 3-D Shapes Surface Area and Volume Lab
practicals.details: Compute surface area/volume of cuboids, cylinders, cones, pyramids, spheres (including hemispheres) using computational thinking.

practicals.title: Prescribed Laboratory Manual Activities
practicals.details: Lab activities from 'Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools' covering empirical probability experiments and theoretical probability concepts.

activities.title: Construct the Square Root Spiral
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral to represent rational and irrational numbers through visual models.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to model patterns in sequences using computational thinking.

activities.title: Visualise Algebraic Identities with Geometric Models
activities.details: Determine factors of algebraic expressions using geometric models and visualising identities.

activities.title: Floor Plan on a Grid
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates to specify locations and positions.

activities.title: Discovering Euclid's Definitions via Sulbasutras
activities.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
activities.details: Describe contributions of India, Egypt, and Greece; explain the construction of a square as described in Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Paper Manipulation for Geometric Patterns
activities.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Involving Circles
activities.details: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, such as the Dharmachakra, Ashoka Chakra, and Sudarshan Chakra.

activities.title: Squaring a Rectangle Proof Activity
activities.chapter_name: Area of shapes, Heron's formula, Squaring a rectangle, Circle area, Brahmagupta's formula
activities.details: Explain the classical problem of 'squaring' a given shape using proof from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Real-life Data Analysis and Graphical Representation
activities.details: Collect, organise, visualise data to answer a statistical investigative question; read stacked bar graphs.

activities.title: Randomness Simulation Experiments
activities.details: Use computational thinking strategies, such as pattern recognition and simulation, to model random experiments.

projects.title: Historical Approximations of Pi
projects.chapter_name: Area of shapes, Heron's formula, Squaring a rectangle, Circle area, Brahmagupta's formula
projects.details: List historical approximations to π from Archimedes, Aryabhata, and Zu Chongzhi; explain ancient civilisations' methods for circle area.

projects.title: Applications of Circles in Transport and Technology
projects.details: Explain how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology; identify cultural motifs.

projects.title: Statistical Investigation Project
projects.details: Apply computational thinking strategies to analyse real-life data, create appropriate graphical representations (stacked bar graphs), and interpret mean, median, mode for decision-making.

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.
Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria.
Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

competency_weightage: Competencies are integrated across all units (CG-1 to CG-11) with specific focus on computational thinking, logical reasoning, and real-life application.
No explicit numerical weightage per competency is provided in the source text; assessment focuses on holistic competency development.

practicals.title: Mathematics Laboratory Activities
practicals.details: Lab activities to be done from prescribed books (NCERT/CBSE) covering representation of rational numbers on number line and density proofs.

practicals.title: Geometric Constructions Lab
practicals.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
practicals.details: Construct geometrical shapes like bisectors of line segments, angles, triangles, and other polygons satisfying given constraints using stated axioms.

practicals.title: 3-D Shapes Surface Area and Volume Lab
practicals.details: Compute surface area/volume of cuboids, cylinders, cones, pyramids, spheres (including hemispheres) using computational thinking.

practicals.title: Prescribed Laboratory Manual Activities
practicals.details: Lab activities from 'Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools' covering empirical probability experiments and theoretical probability concepts.

activities.title: Construct the Square Root Spiral
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral to represent rational and irrational numbers through visual models.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to model patterns in sequences using computational thinking.

activities.title: Visualise Algebraic Identities with Geometric Models
activities.details: Determine factors of algebraic expressions using geometric models and visualising identities.

activities.title: Floor Plan on a Grid
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates to specify locations and positions.

activities.title: Discovering Euclid's Definitions via Sulbasutras
activities.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
activities.details: Describe contributions of India, Egypt, and Greece; explain the construction of a square as described in Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Paper Manipulation for Geometric Patterns
activities.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Involving Circles
activities.details: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, such as the Dharmachakra, Ashoka Chakra, and Sudarshan Chakra.

activities.title: Squaring a Rectangle Proof Activity
activities.chapter_name: Area of shapes, Heron's formula, Squaring a rectangle, Circle area, Brahmagupta's formula
activities.details: Explain the classical problem of 'squaring' a given shape using proof from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Real-life Data Analysis and Graphical Representation
activities.details: Collect, organise, visualise data to answer a statistical investigative question; read stacked bar graphs.

activities.title: Randomness Simulation Experiments
activities.details: Use computational thinking strategies, such as pattern recognition and simulation, to model random experiments.

projects.title: Historical Approximations of Pi
projects.chapter_name: Area of shapes, Heron's formula, Squaring a rectangle, Circle area, Brahmagupta's formula
projects.details: List historical approximations to π from Archimedes, Aryabhata, and Zu Chongzhi; explain ancient civilisations' methods for circle area.

projects.title: Applications of Circles in Transport and Technology
projects.details: Explain how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology; identify cultural motifs.

projects.title: Statistical Investigation Project
projects.details: Apply computational thinking strategies to analyse real-life data, create appropriate graphical representations (stacked bar graphs), and interpret mean, median, mode for decision-making.

Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.
Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria.
Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

competency_groups.title: Understands numbers and number sets
competency_groups.description: Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.
competencies: C-1.1: Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.

competency_groups.title: Builds deductive and inductive logic for proofs
competencies: C-2.1: Understanding of powers (radical powers) and exponents.

competency_groups.title: Discovers algebraic identities and models real-life situations
competency_groups.description: States and proves remainder theorem, factor theorem, division algorithm; models contextualised problems using equations.
competencies: C-3.1: States and proves remainder theorem, factor theorem, and division algorithm.

C-3.2: Models and solves contextualised problems using equations (for example, simultaneous linear equations in two variables or single polynomial equations), and draws conclusions about a situation being modelled.

competency_groups.title: Analyses characteristics of 2D geometric shapes and develops arguments
competency_groups.description: Describes relationships including congruence; proves theorems using Euclid's axioms for triangles, quadrilaterals, circles.
competencies: C-4.1: Describes relationships including congruence of two-dimensional geometric shapes (such as lines, angles, triangles) to make and test conjectures and solve problems.
C-4.2: Proves theorems using Euclid's axioms and postulates for triangles and quadrilaterals, and applies them to solve geometric problems.

competency_groups.title: Derives and uses formulae for areas, surface area, and volumes
competency_groups.description: Visualises Heron's formula; visualises mathematical thinking to discover formulae for solid objects.
competencies: C-5.1: Visualises, represents, and calculates the area of a triangle using Heron's formula and its generalisation to cyclic quadrilaterals given by Brahmagupta's formula.

competency_groups.title: Analyses and interprets data using statistical concepts and probability
competency_groups.description: Applies measures of central tendencies; applies concepts from probability to solve problems.
competencies: C-6.1: Applies measures of central tendencies, such as mean, median, and mode.

competency_groups.title: Perceives the axiomatic structure of Mathematics
competency_groups.description: Proves mathematical statements; visualises geometric proofs for algebraic identities.
competencies: C-7.1: Proves mathematical statements and carries out geometric constructions using stated assumptions, axioms, postulates, definitions, and mathematics vocabulary.

competency_groups.title: Builds skills in visualisation, optimisation, representation, and modelling
competency_groups.description: Models daily-life phenomena; uses 2D representations of 3D objects.
competencies: C-8.1: Models daily-life phenomena and uses representations, such as graphs, tables, and equations to draw conclusions.

competency_groups.title: Develops computational thinking
competency_groups.description: Decomposes problems; describes sequences of instructions.
competencies: C-9.1: Decomposes a problem into sub-problems.

competency_groups.title: Knows and appreciates contributions of mathematicians from India and around the world
competency_groups.description: Recognises important contributions made by mathematicians in the field.
competencies: C-10.1: Recognises the important contributions made by mathematicians (Indian and others) in the field of Mathematics.

competency_groups.title: Explores connections of Mathematics with other subjects
competency_groups.description: Applies mathematical knowledge and tools to analyse problems in multiple subjects.
competencies: C-11.1: Applies mathematical knowledge and tools to analyse problems or situations in multiple subjects across Science, Social Science, Visual Arts, Music, Vocational Education, and Sports.

Integral part of the learning process moving beyond summative examinations.

activities.title: Construct the Square Root Spiral
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral to represent rational and irrational numbers through visual models.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to model patterns in sequences using computational thinking.

activities.title: Visualise Algebraic Identities with Geometric Models
activities.details: Determine factors of algebraic expressions using geometric models and visualising identities.

activities.title: Floor Plan on a Grid
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates to specify locations and positions.

activities.title: Discovering Euclid's Definitions via Sulbasutras
activities.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
activities.details: Describe contributions of India, Egypt, and Greece; explain the construction of a square as described in Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Paper Manipulation for Geometric Patterns
activities.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Involving Circles
activities.details: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, such as the Dharmachakra, Ashoka Chakra, and Sudarshan Chakra.

activities.title: Squaring a Rectangle Proof Activity
activities.chapter_name: Area of shapes, Heron's formula, Squaring a rectangle, Circle area, Brahmagupta's formula
activities.details: Explain the classical problem of 'squaring' a given shape using proof from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Real-life Data Analysis and Graphical Representation
activities.details: Collect, organise, visualise data to answer a statistical investigative question; read stacked bar graphs.

activities.title: Randomness Simulation Experiments
activities.details: Use computational thinking strategies, such as pattern recognition and simulation, to model random experiments.

projects.title: Historical Approximations of Pi
projects.chapter_name: Area of shapes, Heron's formula, Squaring a rectangle, Circle area, Brahmagupta's formula
projects.details: List historical approximations to π from Archimedes, Aryabhata, and Zu Chongzhi; explain ancient civilisations' methods for circle area.

projects.title: Applications of Circles in Transport and Technology
projects.details: Explain how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology; identify cultural motifs.

projects.title: Statistical Investigation Project
projects.details: Apply computational thinking strategies to analyse real-life data, create appropriate graphical representations (stacked bar graphs), and interpret mean, median, mode for decision-making.

practicals.title: Mathematics Laboratory Activities
practicals.details: Lab activities to be done from prescribed books (NCERT/CBSE) covering representation of rational numbers on number line and density proofs.

practicals.title: Geometric Constructions Lab
practicals.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
practicals.details: Construct geometrical shapes like bisectors of line segments, angles, triangles, and other polygons satisfying given constraints using stated axioms.

practicals.title: 3-D Shapes Surface Area and Volume Lab
practicals.details: Compute surface area/volume of cuboids, cylinders, cones, pyramids, spheres (including hemispheres) using computational thinking.

practicals.title: Prescribed Laboratory Manual Activities
practicals.details: Lab activities from 'Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools' covering empirical probability experiments and theoretical probability concepts.

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.
Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria.
Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

competency_weightage: Competencies are integrated across all units (CG-1 to CG-11) with specific focus on computational thinking, logical reasoning, and real-life application.
No explicit numerical weightage per competency is provided in the source text; assessment focuses on holistic competency development.
